description: Configuring a peripheral's registers before enabling its clock or releasing its reset, so the writes are silently ignored.
body: Failure mode: code writes a peripheral's configuration registers following an example, but the peripheral clock was never enabled (or reset not released), so the bus drops the writes; the peripheral stays in its default state and the driver behaves erratically with no error. Detection signals: register read-back does not match what was written; peripheral does nothing despite 'correct' config code; reordering the clock-enable first makes it work. Do not write peripheral registers before clocking the peripheral. Safer fallback: follow the datasheet's exact init order — enable clock, release reset, then configure — read back status/ready bits to confirm each step, and verify bus activity on a logic analyzer.
